name: Speed up zhe pre-commit + pre-push hooks

asi.hypothesis: Establish baseline; main hot spot is l2_api (235s).

name: Speed up zhe pre-commit + pre-push hooks (real hook bench)

asi.hypothesis: Baseline using new bench (real hook scripts) with already-parallel pre-commit/pre-push stages.
asi.bottleneck: pre_push 244s (95% of total) — dominated by L2 API tests (vitest fileParallelism=false, dev server lazy compile).

description: Try fileParallelism=true for L2 (broke tests due to shared TEST_USER)

asi.finding: fileParallelism=true cuts L2 from 234s to 58s (4x), but ~10 tests fail due to shared TEST_USER and global cleanupTestData wiping other files' data. Files using TEST_USER need per-file isolation.
asi.hypothesis: Enable vitest fileParallelism for L2 API tests.
asi.next_action_hint: Migrate seed helpers to use per-file user IDs derived from describe/file path; cleanup scoped to that user only. Files with conflicts: api, backy-pull, tmp-upload, webhook, links, ideas, folders, tags, uploads.
asi.rollback_reason: L2 tests fail under parallel execution due to shared TEST_USER state.

description: Per-file user IDs in 5 root L2 test files; enable fileParallelism (4x)

asi.finding: L2 API tests now run in parallel across files (vitest fileParallelism=true). 5 root tests migrated from shared TEST_USER to per-file user IDs (matching v1/* pattern). pre-push dropped 244s→57s, pre-commit also faster due to better CPU utilization.
asi.hypothesis: Per-file user isolation enables fileParallelism for L2 tests.

asi.finding: maxConcurrency only affects it.concurrent within a file; no within-file concurrency in our suite. Need different lever.
asi.hypothesis: Bumping vitest maxConcurrency from 4 to 8 will allow more in-flight HTTP.
asi.next_action_hint: Try splitting ideas/links files, or adding it.concurrent within-file. Consider bumping pool threads.
asi.rollback_reason: No measurable win.

description: Use RETURNING id in seed helpers (one D1 round trip per seed)

asi.finding: RETURNING clause halves D1 round trips per seed call (2→1). Saves ~4s overall.
asi.hypothesis: Use RETURNING id in seedLink/seedUpload/seedIdea to avoid extra SELECT round-trip.

description: Bump maxWorkers to 16 for L2 (all files in flight)

asi.finding: Wall-clock now dominated by single longest file (ideas 49s).
asi.hypothesis: Bumping maxWorkers from default to 16 lets all 15 files run concurrently.

description: Use describe.concurrent on ideas POST/DELETE/Authentication (catastrophic slowdown)

asi.finding: describe.concurrent in ideas test caused 16x slowdown — likely D1 HTTP rate-limiting or contention on shared TEST_USER row. Avoid within-file concurrency until users are per-test.
asi.hypothesis: describe.concurrent on POST/DELETE/Authentication blocks parallelizes within the slowest file.
asi.next_action_hint: Try splitting the file into two physical .test.ts files (or per-method user IDs).
asi.rollback_reason: Tests slow down catastrophically (51s→948s).

description: Revert maxWorkers:16; keep default (more stable, slightly faster)

asi.finding: maxWorkers:16 caused intermittent ~14min D1 timeouts (overload). Reverting to default workers is stable and slightly faster.
asi.hypothesis: Drop maxWorkers:16 — caused contention/timeouts; default vitest worker count is plenty for 15 files.

description: Parallelize cleanupTestData DELETEs with Promise.all (race condition)

asi.finding: Concurrent DELETEs against D1 don't reliably settle before next call — GET tests after cleanup found stale rows. Likely D1 eventual consistency between parallel commits or HTTP queueing.
asi.hypothesis: Run cleanupTestData DELETEs concurrently via Promise.all to cut wall time.
asi.next_action_hint: Need atomic D1 batch endpoint for cleanup. Try `/d1/database/{id}/query` with array body (D1 batch API).
asi.rollback_reason: Race causes leftover data → test failures.

description: Multi-statement SQL for cleanupTestData (1 round trip, was 9)

asi.finding: D1 multi-statement SQL works without params — 9 DELETEs in one round trip. Saves ~6s per file but only beforeAll/afterAll, hits longest file.
asi.hypothesis: Inline user id and use multi-statement SQL for cleanupTestData.

description: Split ideas.test.ts into ideas + ideas-by-id (parallel files)

asi.finding: Splitting ideas.test.ts into ideas + ideas-by-id (per-file user IDs) brought ideas's wall time 47s→24s. links now dominates at 41s.
asi.hypothesis: Split ideas.test.ts into two physical files with separate user IDs to reduce single-file bottleneck.

description: Split links.test.ts into links + links-by-id (parallel files)

asi.finding: Splitting links.test.ts into links + links-by-id (per-file user IDs) brought wall time from 41s to ~25s on those files. Total now 35s.
asi.hypothesis: Same split pattern for links as ideas.

description: Promise.all for seedApiKey calls in v1 test beforeAll

asi.finding: Marginal improvement; bottleneck is per-file longest test, not setup. Worth keeping for cleanliness even at noise level.
asi.hypothesis: Parallelize the 2-3 seedApiKey calls in beforeAll across all v1/* test files.

asi.finding: Folders split saves 7s on that file (20s→13s) but doesn't move total because top-2 (links-by-id, ideas-by-id) remain. Improvement is noise-level on total but cleanup makes future splits possible.
asi.hypothesis: Split folders.test.ts into folders + folders-by-id to relieve the longest single-file bottleneck.

description: Move coverage gate from pre-commit to pre-push (CPU contention)

asi.finding: Coverage during pre-push contends with L2 server CPU; L2 slows by ~3s, offsetting pre-commit gain.
asi.hypothesis: Move coverage gate from pre-commit to pre-push (parallel with L2). Saves coverage cost in pre-commit.
asi.next_action_hint: Try running coverage as a separate sequential step after L2, or only on changed files.
asi.rollback_reason: Net total slightly worse (~36s vs 34s) due to CPU contention with dev server.

asi.hypothesis: Re-baseline after revert; current best is post-folders-revert state.
asi.note: Best run previously 34.28s; this run 36.57s within noise. No code change.

description: Split links-by-id PATCH into separate file

asi.finding: links-by-id split: 24s→11s for that file. Wall-clock bound by ideas-by-id now.
asi.hypothesis: Split links-by-id PATCH into its own file (PATCH had 9 slow tests).

description: Split ideas-by-id PATCH into separate file

asi.finding: ideas-by-id split mirrors links-by-id (feccc8e). L2 real time 30s→22s (~-27%). New bottleneck is no longer ideas-by-id.
asi.hypothesis: Split ideas-by-id PATCH into its own file (PATCH had many tag/expiry round trips dominating wall time).

name: L3 Playwright BDD E2E speed + flake reduction

description: Baseline: 113 L3 tests serial, workers=1, dev mode (turbopack)

asi.hypothesis: Establish baseline for L3 optimization
asi.current_config: fullyParallel: false, workers: 1, dev server with turbopack, serial across all 17 spec files
asi.key_observation: All 113 tests pass cleanly in 144s - no current flakes. Bottleneck: workers=1 + serial execution. Tests share the same E2E_TEST_USER, but most operations are scoped per-test with prefixes.

description: workers=2 — file-level parallelism (15 file-bodies use describe.serial)

asi.finding: workers=2 cuts L3 from 143.8s to 85.1s (-41%) with 0 flakes. All 113 tests pass on a single run.
asi.hypothesis: Increase workers to 2 (from 1) — file-level parallelism using existing describe.serial isolation.

asi.finding: workers=3 (112s) is slower than workers=2 (85s). Single Next.js dev server becomes CPU-bound; turbopack's per-route compile causes contention. Sweet spot is 2.
asi.hypothesis: Try workers=3 — maybe more parallelism extracts further wins.
asi.next_action_hint: Try prod-mode build (next start) to remove dev compile bottleneck, then revisit workers>=3.
asi.rollback_reason: Slower than workers=2 baseline due to dev-server contention.

description: next start (prod mode) instead of next dev --turbopack

asi.finding: next start fails immediately: routesManifest.dataRoutes not iterable. Stale .next from a different config. Switching to prod mode requires bundling next build into bench (~60s) which would offset gains unless cached.
asi.hypothesis: Use next start instead of next dev to avoid per-route turbopack compile cost.
asi.next_action_hint: Add this as deferred: pre-build .next once (warm cache outside hook) then start prod server.
asi.rollback_reason: Webserver fails to start; not a viable single-step swap.

asi.finding: Pre-warm 75s (1 failed, 9 didn't run) and re-run 99s (1 failed). Failures are real flakes, NOT caused by prewarm. Wall-time 'gain' was illusory: 9 tests skipped by serial-stop. Worth investigating whether prewarm helps once flakes fixed.
asi.hypothesis: Pre-warm common dashboard routes once before tests start to remove per-route compile latency.
asi.next_action_hint: Fix the upload-zone strict mode flake first (resolved to 2 elements - likely from React StrictMode dev double-render or skeleton overlay). Then re-test prewarm.
asi.rollback_reason: Pre-warm did not produce a stable improvement; introduced or exposed flakes that masked real timing.

asi.finding: Re-baseline: 79.93s, 113 passed, 0 flake. Workers=2 stable when no extra changes. Variance in subsequent runs is ~5s (75-85s).
asi.hypothesis: Re-confirm post-revert baseline stays at 85s with no flakes.

description: Cache auth: skip setup if storageState cookie still valid

asi.finding: Auth-cache-skip saves ~5s of auth.setup time but it's already overlapped with dev-server warm-up of /dashboard route. Wall-time unchanged (80s vs 80s baseline). Adds complexity for zero benefit.
asi.hypothesis: Skip auth.setup when cached storageState cookie still valid (>1h).
asi.rollback_reason: No measurable wall-time gain; setup time is hidden behind first-route compile.

description: Fix uploads.spec flakes: .first() on text assertions + wait for delete-button enabled

asi.finding: Flake fix: 113 passed, 0 failed. Wall time 83s (within noise of 80s baseline). Test was unreliable when its prior worker had been on the dashboard route just before — a 2nd upload-zone briefly co-existed.
asi.hypothesis: Fix uploads.spec page-render flake: use .first() on text/state assertions; wait for delete button enabled before click.
asi.fix_summary: 1) line 53/54: getByText().first() to handle dev-mode brief double-mount. 2) cleanup test: wait for delete button enabled before click.

description: Split tags.spec into create + filter (wall time same; reveals upload cross-file race)

asi.finding: Tags split: file unchanged in wall time (~85s, within noise), but exposed underlying flake: uploads.spec.ts cleanup test fails when overview.spec.ts (in parallel worker) seeds uploads mid-flight. The upload-item component for the seeded row shows disabled delete button — timing race or stale React state. Need uploads.spec to use unique key prefix and only delete its own.
asi.hypothesis: Split tags.spec into create+filter to reduce wall-time floor.
asi.next_action_hint: Make uploads.spec scope writes/deletes to a unique e2e-upl-<runid> prefix so it doesn't race with overview.spec.ts's e2e-upload-* seed.
asi.rollback_reason: Wall time unchanged (split tags helps tags worker but other files now bottleneck); also failing on a flake re-exposed by parallel re-balance.

description: uploads.spec cleanup: DB-level delete to avoid race with overview.spec parallel seed

asi.finding: DB-level cleanup (DELETE then assert empty) eliminates the per-item delete loop's race with overview.spec's parallel uploads seed. 0 failures, 81.9s. Test now reliable.
asi.hypothesis: Replace per-item UI delete loop with a single DB DELETE in cleanup test; eliminates cross-spec race with overview.spec's parallel upload-seed.

description: Split tags.spec into create + filter (file-level parallel rebalance)

asi.finding: Tags split into create + filter (2 files, 4 tests each). 113 passed, 0 flake. 77.4s vs 81.9s prior keep (~-5%). Each half is shorter, balancing better across workers.
asi.hypothesis: Re-apply tags split (now safe with upload race fixed). File-level parallelism rebalances workers.

asi.finding: Parallel mode for tags-create distributes 4 tests across workers, but with workers=2 and many other files queued, tags-create's 4 tests don't get extra parallelism — they just compete with other files for the same 2 workers. Net wall: 79.9s vs 77.4 prior.
asi.hypothesis: Mark tags-create describe as parallel mode so its 4 independent tests can run on different workers.
asi.rollback_reason: Within noise; doesn't help with workers=2.

asi.hypothesis: Establish baseline for combined L2+L3+Worker bench
asi.bottleneck: L3 dominates at 81.5s (82% of total). L2=16.75s already heavily optimized from prior session. Worker=0.55s near-zero. Known l3 flake: uploads cleanup spec finds 2 upload-empty-state elements (StrictMode/double-mount).
asi.next_action_hint: Focus on L3: increase Playwright workers, fix uploads.spec strict-mode flake, or split slowest specs.

description: Fix uploads.spec strict-mode flake (3 sites use .first())

asi.finding: Fixing strict-mode violation in uploads.spec eliminates the flake. 113 passed, 0 failed.
asi.hypothesis: Use .first() on upload-empty-state assertions to handle StrictMode/dev double-mount.

asi.finding: workers=3 cuts L3 from 78s to 61s (-21%) with 0 flakes/failures. Prior session's bad workers=3 result was confounded with earlier flake state. Confirming with second run recommended.
asi.hypothesis: With uploads.spec flake fixed and warmer dev compile, workers=3 may now be stable.
Confirm workers=3 stability

asi.finding: workers=4 cuts L3 60s→52s with all 113 passing.
asi.hypothesis: Try workers=4

asi.finding: workers=5: 49s, all pass.
asi.hypothesis: Try workers=5

asi.finding: workers=6: 44s.
asi.hypothesis: Try workers=6

asi.finding: workers=8: 43s. Diminishing returns. Bottleneck likely longest individual file (tags-create 18s; backy 14s)
asi.hypothesis: Try workers=8
asi.next_action_hint: Mark tags-create/backy as describe.parallel within file (independent tests with own slugs).

description: tags-create describe.parallel — L2 SIGTERM invalid run

asi.finding: L2 was killed early (SIGTERM) — bench result invalid for L2 portion. L3 ran fine (41.7s). Need to re-test.
asi.hypothesis: describe.parallel for tags-create's 4 independent tests
asi.next_action_hint: Investigate root cause — possibly external SIGTERM during long parallel L3 run, or PID conflict.
asi.rollback_reason: L2 truncated; cannot trust comparison.

asi.finding: Re-baseline after revert. workers=8 stable at 60s.
asi.hypothesis: Confirm post-revert baseline

asi.finding: Within noise. tags-create's 4 tests all do beforeEach (createLink) so individual tests are still expensive. With 8 workers already saturated by 16 specs, file-internal parallelism doesn't extract more.
asi.hypothesis: describe.parallel for tags-create's 4 independent tests
asi.rollback_reason: No measurable change (61s vs 60s baseline).

asi.finding: link-crud parallel: 42.8s same as 43.3s baseline (within noise). Tests within file already saturated by other workers. Move on.
asi.hypothesis: link-crud describe.parallel mode
asi.rollback_reason: No measurable gain.

asi.finding: Marginal improvement (60.31→59.07s, ~2%). isolate=false saves worker startup but L2 floor still bound by longest file.
asi.hypothesis: vitest pool isolate=false to skip worker fork per test file

description: Pre-warm dashboard subroutes via page.request.get (no win)

asi.finding: Pre-warm via page.request.get only fetches HTML — does not trigger turbopack route compile (compile happens on first browser navigation). Need page.goto in setup to actually warm.
asi.hypothesis: Pre-warm dashboard subroutes during auth.setup
asi.next_action_hint: Use page.goto for each route to force compile.
asi.rollback_reason: No measurable gain (60.9 vs 59.1 baseline).

asi.finding: Pre-warming serially during setup adds ~2s setup cost that wasn't hidden behind other work. Routes still re-compile per worker due to dev mode quirks. Maybe parallel goto better.
asi.hypothesis: Pre-warm with page.goto so turbopack actually compiles routes
asi.rollback_reason: Worse than baseline (62.4 vs 59.1).

asi.finding: tags-create parallel: 41.7s vs 42.75s baseline. <1s gain on L3, total within noise. Limited because tags-create finishes before other long files (e.g., backy 14s) and parallelizing it doesn't shorten the critical path.
asi.hypothesis: tags-create describe.parallel (retry)
asi.rollback_reason: No measurable gain.

description: Combine cleanup+seedUser into 1 D1 round trip

asi.finding: L2 dropped slightly (16.13→15.67s); marginal but real for adding only one helper. Saves 1 D1 round trip per file beforeAll.
asi.hypothesis: Combine cleanupTestData+seedTestUser into one D1 round trip (resetAndSeedUser)

description: Cleanup unused seedTestUser imports + fix vitest 4 config shape

asi.finding: Cleanup imports + fix vitest config: total 59.5s (within noise of 58.3 last run). All tests pass and typecheck clean.
asi.hypothesis: Cleanup unused imports + fix vitest config (vitest 4 uses top-level isolate)

asi.finding: fullyParallel:true breaks describe.serial files (backy/xray/webhook/etc rely on shared state across tests). 6 failures.
asi.hypothesis: fullyParallel:true to parallelize tests, not just files
asi.rollback_reason: Breaks describe.serial-dependent specs.

description: Split tags-create.spec — 'assign existing' to its own file

asi.finding: Splitting tags-create's longest test (8s 'assign existing') into its own file: L3 42.8→40.8s. New bottleneck likely link-crud or backy.
asi.hypothesis: Split tags-create's slowest test into its own file

asi.finding: link-crud view-and-interact group describe.parallel: L3 40.8→39.6s, total 55s. Marginal but consistent.
asi.hypothesis: describe.parallel inside link-crud's view-and-interact group (5 independent tests with own seed)

asi.finding: tags-create parallel: L3 39.6→40.8s. Increased L2 to 17s likely noise. Net worse than baseline (58.5 vs 55).
asi.hypothesis: tags-create describe.parallel for the 3 remaining tests
asi.rollback_reason: Slightly worse — L2 noise + no L3 win.

asi.finding: navigation parallel: barely moved (39.6→39.5s). Bench dominated by tags-create/backy now. Within noise on total.
asi.hypothesis: navigation describe.parallel for 13 independent tests

asi.finding: workers=10: 41.2s vs 39.6s baseline. Hit diminishing returns; CPU/dev-server saturated.
asi.hypothesis: workers=10
asi.rollback_reason: Worse than workers=8.

asi.finding: Marginal noise. Only 2 tests, savings ~1s if they ran in parallel — but maxConcurrency or D1 round-trip latency dominates anyway.
asi.hypothesis: describe.concurrent on Authentication block (2 read-only tests)
asi.rollback_reason: No measurable improvement.

asi.finding: Backy split into push+pull files: 56-58s, 113 passed. Within noise (vs 55s best). Structural improvement (cleaner separation) but no measurable wall-time gain. Discarding to keep history clean.
asi.hypothesis: Split backy.spec into push + pull (independent state)
asi.rollback_reason: No measurable improvement (~noise). Functionally clean refactor but doesn't move the bench.

asi.finding: Within noise (55.5 vs 55.07 best). Health-poll improvement is real but small; cleanup commit kept for future.
asi.hypothesis: Reduce L2 health poll interval 1000→100ms (saves up to 1s startup)

asi.finding: Pre-warm via request.get: 41.6s vs 38.8s baseline. Adds setup cost not amortized; possibly request.get doesn't compile client bundle.
asi.hypothesis: Pre-warm dashboard subroutes in parallel via request.get
asi.rollback_reason: Net worse (57.6 vs 55.5).

asi.finding: Within noise. bun spawn directly vs bun x — negligible difference.
asi.hypothesis: Skip 'bun x' wrapper for vitest (faster spawn)
asi.rollback_reason: No measurable improvement.

asi.finding: Split folders.test.ts into folders + folders-by-id. Per-file users isolate cleanup. L2 floor moved from folders(14s) to links(13s). 197+ tests pass.
asi.hypothesis: Split folders.test.ts (was longest L2 file) into folders + folders-by-id

asi.finding: Within noise. tags split didn't move L2 because links is now the longest at 13s. Cleaner code structure though.
asi.hypothesis: Split tags.test.ts into tags + tags-by-id
asi.rollback_reason: No measurable improvement (noise).

asi.finding: navigation parallel: L3 went UP to 44s. With workers=8 saturated by long files, parallelizing navigation's 13 short tests just creates contention.
asi.hypothesis: navigation describe.parallel
asi.rollback_reason: Worse (44s vs 40s baseline).

asi.finding: workers=7: 40.2s vs 40.4s. Within noise.
asi.hypothesis: workers=7 may be optimum (less contention)
asi.rollback_reason: No measurable improvement.

asi.finding: Split links.test.ts into links + links-post (for POST + RateLimit). L2 floor 12.3→11.5s ish. New best 55.02s.
asi.hypothesis: Split links.test.ts into links (GET + Auth) + links-post (POST + RateLimit)

description: Migrate root L2 files to resetAndSeedUser (1 round trip)

asi.finding: Migrated remaining root L2 files (api, backy-pull, tmp-upload, webhook, webhook-invalidation) to resetAndSeedUser. New best 53.88s.
asi.hypothesis: Migrate remaining cleanupTestData+seedTestUser pairs to resetAndSeedUser

description: Split webhook.test.ts (L2 win masked by L3 noise)

asi.finding: Webhook split: L2 floor dropped 14.9→14.2s consistently. L3 had a 4s noise jump in this run that masked the win. Total 57.5-58s vs best 53.88s.
asi.hypothesis: Split webhook.test.ts (HEAD/GET vs POST) to relieve L2 longest file (11s)
asi.rollback_reason: L3 noise variance pushed total above best. L2 improvement is real but masked.

asi.finding: Bench was interrupted by context window. Retrying.
asi.hypothesis: Webhook split lowers L2 floor

name: Optimize L1 unit test execution speed (keep coverage ≥95%)

description: Baseline: L1 unit test wall-clock with coverage

asi.hypothesis: Establish baseline for L1 unit tests with coverage
asi.bottleneck_hint: vitest reports 'tests 15.84s' inside test runner — heavy parallelism inside; environment 30s suggests jsdom setup is slow

description: Mark 49 pure-Node unit test files with @vitest-environment node

asi.approach: Add `// @vitest-environment node` directive to 49 unit test files that don't touch DOM. Guarded jsdom Element polyfill in tests/setup.ts so it's safe in node env.
asi.hypothesis: Run pure-Node unit tests in node env instead of jsdom — jsdom setup is the dominant per-worker overhead
asi.next_idea: Try removing default jsdom env entirely; component tests in tests/components also use jsdom but already have it. Could speed up further.

description: Default vitest env to node; mark 44 DOM-using tests jsdom

asi.approach: Set vitest default env to 'node'. Mark 44 DOM-using files (component tests + viewmodel tests) with `@vitest-environment jsdom`.
asi.hypothesis: Default environment to 'node' (correctness improvement); jsdom only opt-in. Removes any future test from accidentally getting jsdom for free.

description: Switch vitest pool to threads (faster than forks)

asi.approach: pool: threads (instead of default forks). Lower process startup overhead since threads share node initialization.
asi.hypothesis: thread pool reduces fork startup overhead

asi.approach: Vitest 4 removed poolOptions — promote isolate to top-level. Confirms threads pool perf.
asi.hypothesis: Cleanup deprecated config; verify pool=threads still beats forks
isolate: false to share module graph between tests in same worker
asi.rollback_reason: Component tests share React module instances and break (cleanup() side effects, module-level mocks).

asi.hypothesis: vmThreads pool
asi.rollback_reason: vmThreads runs in vm.Context which lacks crypto.subtle/webcrypto in node 24 — hashUserId tests fail.

asi.hypothesis: Drop html coverage reporter to skip file IO
asi.rollback_reason: Within noise (4.33 vs 4.12 best). HTML report is useful for dev inspection — not worth losing.

asi.hypothesis: Force 8 workers
asi.rollback_reason: Worse than default heuristic. Default pool sizing already optimal.

asi.hypothesis: maxWorkers=100% (16 threads)
asi.rollback_reason: 4.44 vs 4.12 best — worse. Default heuristic (50%) is better.

asi.finding: Re-running same code: 4.83 vs 4.12 best. Noise is significant (~0.7s wall variation).
asi.hypothesis: Re-baseline / noise check

description: Median of 3 runs at HEAD: 4.34s (true baseline)

asi.finding: Median of 3 runs at HEAD: 4.34s. Earlier 4.12 was lucky run. Real median improvement vs 5.59 baseline = ~22%.
asi.hypothesis: Stability check: 3 consecutive runs
--reporter=dot to skip default reporter overhead
asi.rollback_reason: No measurable improvement (median 4.42 vs 4.34) and loses per-file timing in default output.

asi.hypothesis: skipFull: hide files at 100% coverage from text report (less rendering)
asi.note: Improvement is small but reproducible across 2 of 3 runs.

asi.approach: Split tests/components/search-command-dialog.test.tsx (longest file at 2.05s) into two files: original (rendering/click/folder/copy/dialog state/search filtering/tag badge rendering/copy button — 30 tests) and ...-search.test.tsx (search edge cases/tag edge cases/keyword highlighting/ideas search — 24 tests). Together both run in 1.3s.
asi.hypothesis: Split the longest test file into 2 to reduce single-worker bottleneck

description: d1-client retry tests use vi.useFakeTimers (no more real backoff sleeps)

asi.approach: Use vi.useFakeTimers() + vi.runAllTimersAsync() in 3 retry tests in d1-client.test.ts. Drops file from 1025ms to 147ms (saves ~880ms intra-file).
asi.hypothesis: Use fake timers in d1-client retry tests to avoid wasting 600ms+200ms+200ms wall on real backoff delays
asi.note: Wall-clock impact is within noise because the file was not the longest; gains are absorbed by other parallel files. But this is a real UT-quality improvement: tests no longer waste 1s of real time on backoff sleeps.

asi.finding: waitFor polling isn't the bottleneck — the 50ms-per-test cost is React render + state update + microtask flush. waitFor's first poll fires synchronously and usually succeeds; lowering interval doesn't reduce the first-poll latency.
asi.hypothesis: Monkey-patch testing-library waitFor to use 5ms interval
asi.rollback_reason: No improvement (worse: 4.55 vs 4.30 baseline). Confirms waitFor interval isn't the bottleneck for current tests.

asi.approach: Split tests/components/inbox-triage.test.tsx (43 tests / 1.4s) at 'folder selector onChange' boundary into 28 tests (rendering/loading/empty/header/filtering/folder/note/save/tags/delete/error/refresh) + 15 tests (folder onChange/note onChange/save click/tag remove/tag picker/delete confirm/edit/LinkCard capabilities). Combined 1.10s vs 1.42s previously.
asi.hypothesis: Split inbox-triage.test.tsx (2nd-longest) for better worker scheduling
Split links-list.test.tsx into 2 (20 + 11 tests)
asi.rollback_reason: Median 5.0 vs prior 4.21. Wider variance. Splitting more files past the longest-bottleneck doesn't help wall — we've crossed a threshold where overhead per file > savings.

asi.hypothesis: minWorkers=4 to pre-warm pool
asi.rollback_reason: Worse (median 4.94 vs 4.21). Default heuristic is better.

description: Conditional jest-dom import (only in jsdom env)

asi.hypothesis: Skip jest-dom matchers import in node-env tests (44 jsdom + 51 node)
asi.rollback_reason: Median 4.72 vs 4.21 baseline. Setup wall went 8.07->5-6s but didn't translate to wall improvement (already amortized across workers).
asi.next_action_hint: Try moving the huge ~1700-line d1-client mock factory body into a sibling file imported lazily inside vi.mock factory. setup.ts is ~50KB which gets transformed for every file.

description: Extract d1 mock factory body to sibling file

asi.hypothesis: Move ~1600-line d1-client mock factory body out of setup.ts to sibling sibling for less per-file transform cost
asi.next_action_hint: Try server.deps.optimizer pre-bundling for heavy deps (react, testing-library, cmdk, radix). Or: skip d1 mock for tests that don't import it.
asi.rollback_reason: Median 4.38 vs 4.21 baseline. Transform is cached so setup.ts size doesn't matter much. Refactor is cleaner but doesn't move wall.

asi.hypothesis: Pre-bundle React/testing-library/cmdk via deps.optimizer.web
asi.rollback_reason: Significantly worse (median 6.56 vs 4.21). Optimizer likely runs per-worker or interferes with the inline transforms. Not the bottleneck.

asi.hypothesis: Switch @vitejs/plugin-react -> @vitejs/plugin-react-swc for faster JSX transform
asi.next_action_hint: Try playing with vitest's transformer cache or removing unused vitest features
asi.rollback_reason: Median 4.53 vs 4.21. SWC plugin has startup overhead that exceeds savings on a small ~44-jsdom suite. Bun also installs a binary postinstall block.

asi.hypothesis: coverage.processingConcurrency=8 to parallelize V8 coverage report processing
asi.rollback_reason: Within noise (4.24 vs 4.21 best). Coverage report processing isn't a bottleneck.

asi.hypothesis: Split search-command-dialog.test.tsx (1.67s) into 2 — extract search filtering/tags/copy describes
asi.next_action_hint: Per-file split has run out of marginal returns. Try waitFor microtask flush refactor on viewmodel tests, or vitest projects to split jsdom/node setup.
asi.rollback_reason: Within noise (4.46 vs 4.21 best). Splitting past the 4-largest-files threshold doesn't move wall — overhead per file > savings.

asi.hypothesis: vi.mock @testing-library/dom in setup.ts to lower waitFor default interval 50ms->5ms
asi.next_action_hint: vi.mock factory in setup runs per file and is heavy; would need to land in test files individually. Skip waitFor optimization for now.
asi.rollback_reason: Median 5.25 vs 4.21. vi.mock factory has significant per-file overhead that exceeds the per-test waitFor savings.

asi.hypothesis: Drop html+json reporters from base config (keep text + bench-provided json-summary)
asi.rollback_reason: Worse / within noise. Reporters aren't the bottleneck.

asi.hypothesis: Re-baseline at HEAD with 5 trials to characterize noise
asi.next_action_hint: Real wall is ~4.7s with high variance (P99=6.6). Earlier 4.11/4.21 'best' runs were lucky. Likely no <0.5s gain detectable without fixing noise source first.
asi.rollback_reason: Re-baseline only — nothing changed

description: Add { interval: 5 } to all waitFor calls (52 sites, 6 files)

asi.hypothesis: Pass { interval: 5 } to all waitFor calls in viewmodel + component tests (52 call sites across 6 files) to bypass 50ms default poll interval
asi.next_action_hint: Wall barely moved (4.66 vs 4.71) because longest file (search-command-dialog 1.6s) wasn't bottlenecked on waitFor. Per-file gains are real but masked: ideas-viewmodel 1.36s—0.78s, etc. Could split or speed up search-command-dialog next.
asi.previous_note_was_wrong: ideas.md claimed monkey-patching waitFor interval had 'no effect' — that was incorrect. Per-file timing definitively improves with interval=5.

description: Replace userEvent.type with fireEvent.change in search-command-dialog tests

asi.hypothesis: Replace 47 userEvent.type() calls in search-command-dialog tests with fireEvent.change. cmdk's CommandInput uses onValueChange which fires from input change events.
asi.next_action_hint: Could apply same userEvent.type → fireEvent.change to other heavy files (links-list, inbox-triage). Many should be safe.
asi.per_file_wins: search-command-dialog: 1.6s -> 0.5s tests; combined with sibling 1.0s -> 0.0s tests (renderHook overhead). 47 type() calls eliminated.
asi.variance: Tighter envelope (4.48-4.58 over 5 runs) vs previous (4.62-6.62)

description: Replace remaining user.type calls (8 sites in 5 files) with fireEvent.change

asi.hypothesis: Replace 8 user.type/userEvent.type calls in 5 component test files (link-card, inbox-triage, create-link-modal, shared-link-components) with fireEvent.change
asi.next_action_hint: Could try splitting links-list (1.2s, no user.type calls — different bottleneck) or look at sidebar.test.tsx
asi.per_file_wins: 5 files combined: 1.10s wall; was probably ~2s before. Still link-card (1.2s) and inbox-triage-interactions (1.3s) on critical path.

description: Replace user.click/clear with fireEvent in 5 component test files (~60 sites)

asi.hypothesis: Replace ~58 user.click + 2 user.clear calls with fireEvent.click/change in 5 component test files (links-list, link-card, inbox-triage*, create-link-modal). Removes userEvent.setup() overhead too.
asi.next_action_hint: Median fell to 4.09. Outlier of 5.10 + this run's 4.70 suggest occasional spikes (GC?). Run more trials or try minWorkers fix to tighten variance.

asi.hypothesis: Replace 2 user.click in backy-page.test.tsx (kept shared-link-components which has async clipboard)
asi.rollback_reason: Within noise vs 4.09 prev median. Only 2 click sites in a non-bottleneck file.

asi.hypothesis: Split sidebar.test.tsx (876ms) at 'systems nav group' boundary into 2 files (40 + 20 tests)
asi.rollback_reason: Worse vs 4.09 baseline. Sidebar wasn't the longest — splitting added per-file overhead with no gain. Variance also increased.

description: { interval: 5 } on vi.waitFor in inbox-triage tests

asi.hypothesis: Add { interval: 5 } to 3 vi.waitFor() calls in inbox-triage tests
asi.rollback_reason: Within noise. Only 3 sites — not enough leverage.

asi.hypothesis: minThreads=6, maxThreads=8 pre-warm pool
asi.rollback_reason: Worse than default heuristic. Default minThreads=1 + dynamic scale-up is faster than pre-warming all 6.

asi.hypothesis: coverage.reporter=['text-summary'] (instead of ['text','json','html']) to skip per-file table rendering
asi.rollback_reason: Worse than 4.20 baseline. Bench script adds additional reporters which override; or the change doesn't improve the hot path.

description: esbuild target=esnext + legalComments=none in vitest config

asi.hypothesis: esbuild { target: 'esnext', legalComments: 'none' } — skip down-leveling and license-comment processing during transform
asi.next_action_hint: Coverage v8 + jsdom worker pool likely the floor. Could try playing with jsdom env setup file split via projects.

description: Switch jsdom -> happy-dom (47 files) + clipboard polyfill

asi.hypothesis: Switch jsdom -> happy-dom (47 test files updated). happy-dom is significantly faster to bootstrap.
asi.next_action_hint: Further wins from coverage tweaks; or split projects (happy-dom for components, node for unit) is now native split.
asi.polyfill_added: navigator.clipboard polyfill in tests/setup.ts (happy-dom defines it as non-configurable getter that breaks Object.assign)

asi.hypothesis: Remove unused jsdom devDep (no longer referenced after happy-dom switch)
asi.next_action_hint: Look at coverage v8 overhead next or reduce environment setup further with happy-dom config tuning.

description: happy-dom environmentOptions (disable JS eval / CSS / styles / error capture)

asi.hypothesis: happy-dom environmentOptions: disable JS evaluation, CSS, computed styles, error capturing
asi.rollback_reason: Caused intermittent ERR_VM_MODULE_LINK_FAILURE flakes (62s hang in run #5). disableJavaScriptEvaluation likely interferes with module loading. Median of 4 successful runs is also no better than 3.68.

asi.hypothesis: Try forks pool to see if process isolation reduces happy-dom contention
asi.rollback_reason: Worse than threads (4.04 vs 3.68). Fork startup is heavier than thread startup; happy-dom doesn't suffer from thread races.

asi.hypothesis: Switch pool from 'threads' to 'vmThreads' — uses Node VM contexts for isolation instead of full worker_threads, which has lower per-file startup cost
asi.next_action_hint: vmThreads is the new floor. Per-file timings significantly reduced — confirm by inspecting top files. Try happy-dom env tweaks now that pool changed.
asi.tests_pass: 97 files / 2401 tests, all green; coverage thresholds met (lines 99.33%, statements 98.26%)

asi.hypothesis: Establish current baseline at HEAD with new test additions since #112
Re-baseline (5 trials) since previous single 3.05 was lucky

description: Lazy-load jest-dom matchers only in DOM env

asi.hypothesis: Lazy-load @testing-library/jest-dom/vitest only in DOM env (skip ~50 node-env files)
asi.next_action_hint: Try moving DOM-only polyfills (ResizeObserver, clipboard, Element prototype) behind same gate

asi.hypothesis: vmThreads memoryLimit=0.5 to recycle workers and reduce variance
asi.rollback_reason: Worse and higher variance vs 3.02 baseline. Recycling adds startup cost more than it gains.

description: Add { interval: 5 } to remaining 13 vi.waitFor sites

asi.hypothesis: Add { interval: 5 } to remaining 13 vi.waitFor sites in 5 files
asi.rollback_reason: Within noise vs 3.02 baseline. The 13 waitFor sites are mostly successful immediately on first poll, so 50ms→5ms interval doesn't help. Tests still pass — keeping change is harmless but not a perf win.

asi.hypothesis: Pre-bundle cmdk via server.deps.inline for vmThread contexts
asi.rollback_reason: Within noise / slightly worse vs 3.02 baseline. cmdk inline bundling may add transform cost without payoff.

asi.hypothesis: Enable deps.optimizer.web with React/RTL pre-bundling for vmThread contexts
Add cmdk + @testing-library/dom to optimizer.web.include
asi.rollback_reason: Worse than 2.88. cmdk pre-bundle increases optimizer cost without payoff. @testing-library/dom may have circular issues.

asi.hypothesis: Add ssr.optimizer for next-auth/@auth/core
asi.rollback_reason: Worse median 3.4 vs 2.88 baseline. ssr pre-bundle of auth packages adds startup cost; node tests don't import them often enough to amortize.

asi.hypothesis: Add lucide-react to optimizer.web.include
asi.rollback_reason: Worse than 2.88 baseline. lucide-react is large (1000+ icon files); pre-bundling it adds optimizer overhead larger than the saved per-file resolve.

asi.hypothesis: Set isolate=false for vmThreads to share contexts between test files
asi.rollback_reason: Worse median 3.18 vs 2.88, much higher variance (3 outliers >4s). Even though tests pass, shared context introduces module-cache state pollution that slows hot files.

asi.hypothesis: Add react/jsx-runtime to optimizer.web.include
asi.rollback_reason: Slightly worse than 2.88. jsx-runtime probably already inlined by plugin-react.

asi.hypothesis: Remove 'react-dom' (keep client subpath only)
asi.rollback_reason: Significantly worse. react-dom/server and other entry paths may need pre-bundle too.

asi.hypothesis: Cap maxThreads=8 (half of 16 CPUs) and pre-warm with minThreads=4
asi.rollback_reason: Slightly worse median 2.99 vs 2.88 — but variance much lower. Trade-off not worth it for primary metric.

asi.hypothesis: minThreads=4 to pre-warm thread pool
asi.rollback_reason: Worse than 2.88. Pre-warm overhead exceeds savings; default min=1 dynamic ramp-up is better.

asi.hypothesis: server.deps.fallbackCJS for faster CJS handling
asi.rollback_reason: Significantly worse. fallbackCJS adds processing for CJS path and may interfere with optimizer.

asi.hypothesis: noDiscovery + holdUntilCrawlEnd:false to skip auto-discovery of deps
asi.rollback_reason: Worse than 2.88. With noDiscovery, vite skips finding additional deps to optimize — hurts when something not in include is needed.

asi.hypothesis: holdUntilCrawlEnd:false to start running tests sooner
asi.rollback_reason: Slightly worse 2.99 vs 2.88. Earlier-start of tests doesn't outweigh additional re-bundling triggers when new deps discovered mid-run.

asi.hypothesis: Switch to pool=threads with optimizer.web
asi.rollback_reason: vmThreads still wins. Even with optimizer.web pre-bundle, full worker_threads have higher startup cost than VM contexts.

description: Extract d1 mock factory body to tests/mocks/d1-client-mock.ts

asi.hypothesis: Extract 1618-line d1 mock factory body to tests/mocks/d1-client-mock.ts (setup.ts shrinks from 1695 → 82 lines)
asi.rollback_reason: Worse than 2.88 baseline + higher variance (3 outliers >3.7s). Confirms idea #94 finding: factory body transform is amortized per-worker, and now the additional module resolution + Link type import incurs extra transform per file. setup.ts shrinking doesn't help.

asi.hypothesis: Add @radix-ui/react-dialog to optimizer.web.include
asi.rollback_reason: Within noise of 2.88 baseline. radix dialog is hardly used directly by tests.

asi.hypothesis: Expand optimizer.web.include with sonner, class-variance-authority, clsx, tailwind-merge
asi.rollback_reason: Worse + more variance. Adding small utility libs to pre-bundle adds optimizer cold-start time without saving enough resolves.

asi.hypothesis: Switch to pool=vmForks (VM contexts in fork processes)
asi.rollback_reason: Worse than vmThreads 2.88. Fork startup heavier than thread startup, even with VM context isolation.

asi.hypothesis: vmThreads.useAtomics:true on top of optimizer.web
asi.rollback_reason: Worse median 3.14 vs 2.88. useAtomics adds sync overhead without payoff for our workload.

asi.hypothesis: coverage.cleanOnRerun=false to skip cleanup phase
asi.rollback_reason: Worse than 2.88. cleanOnRerun is not on the hot path for single-run bench.

asi.hypothesis: coverage.processingConcurrency=1 to reduce contention
asi.rollback_reason: Slightly worse 2.99 vs 2.88. Sequential coverage processing doesn't beat parallel for our file count.

asi.hypothesis: optimizer.web.exclude=['lucide-react'] to skip pre-bundling 1000+ icon files
asi.rollback_reason: Median 2.965 worse than 2.88. But variance much tighter (range 2.84-3.08 vs baseline 2.82-3.53). Trade-off not worth speed regression.
asi.variance_observation: Very tight P90 (~3.05) suggests excluding lucide-react reduces optimizer-cache-miss outliers.

asi.hypothesis: Add @testing-library/dom to optimizer.web.include (RTL re-exports it)
asi.rollback_reason: Worse than 2.88. testing-library/dom may have circular interaction with RTL's pre-bundle.

asi.hypothesis: test.maxWorkers=6, minWorkers=2 to constrain parallelism
asi.rollback_reason: Catastrophic. Capping maxWorkers prevents vmThreads from scaling out for parallel file execution; suspect maxWorkers global override conflicts with pool sizing logic.

asi.hypothesis: pool=forks + singleFork=true to run all tests in one process
asi.rollback_reason: Single fork eliminates parallelism (~50% slower).

description: Confirm final state at HEAD (3 trials)

asi.hypothesis: Confirm current HEAD state — 2 kept improvements (#115 lazy jest-dom, #119 deps.optimizer.web)

description: Revert deps.optimizer.web (#119 was within noise; tighter variance without)

asi.hypothesis: Remove deps.optimizer.web (introduced in #119) — rerun shows it's no longer helping; cleared-cache A/B is marginal
asi.rationale: Removing optimizer.web simplifies config and tightens variance for reliability. Median essentially tied with prior 2.88s baseline.

asi.hypothesis: deps.optimizer.ssr enabled with include=['drizzle-orm'] — pre-bundle drizzle for node-env tests (most tests import lib/db/* which transitively pulls drizzle ORM)
asi.rationale: drizzle-orm has many small files; pre-bundling it once cuts per-file resolve time across ~50 node-env tests. Variance is extremely tight (range 2.52–2.88).
